world.title: Which European countries have the most teenage entrepreneurs?
world.description: Around 69,000 EU entrepreneurs are under 20. The Netherlands and Italy lead the pack — a positive sign for Rome, amid gloomy youth employment rates.View on euronews

world.title: Lebanon Accuses Israel of Ecocide Following Massive Environmental Destruction
world.description: Lebanon’s Environment Ministry claims Israel’s military committed "ecocide," destroying 5,000 hectares of forest and causing $700m in agricultural losses.

world.title: PH calls for stronger food security coordination in Asia-Pacific
world.description: The Philippines, through the Department of Agriculture (DA), is calling for stronger regional coordination to ensure food security across the Asia-Pacific as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East disrupt global supply chains, driving up agricultural input costs.

world.title: Europe makes mistake regarding war in Ukraine back in 2022, says Estonian president
world.description: Estonian President Alar Karis stated that Europe missed the opportunity to begin peace talks with Moscow at the start of the full-scale war in Ukraine. — Ukrinform.

world.title: Sri Lanka arrests 22 Buddhist monks allegedly carrying over 110 kilos of cannabis at airport
world.description: Sri Lankan authorities said 22 Buddhist monks were arrested for possession of over 110 kilograms of cannabis – illegal in the South Asian nation – at the international airport in the capital Colombo, the largest ever drug bust at the terminal.

world.title: Jimmy Kimmel defends Melania joke after Trumps call for his sacking
world.description: Kimmel has insisted that the joke was not ‘a call to assassination’ but a ‘light roast’ about the age difference between Trump and his wife

business.title: Tech Drags Stocks Lower as Brent Tops $110 Again: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Technology stocks led equity markets lower as concerns over whether the vast amount of investment in artificial intelligence will pay off resurfaced. Brent rose above $110 a barrel as the Strait of Hormuz stayed shut.

business.title: Malaysia’s data centre capacity to double to 2,055MW by 2026
business.description: Malaysia’s data centre capacity is set to more than double to 2,055 megawatts (MW) by end-2026, positioning Johor as one of the fastest-growing markets in the Asia-Pacific (Apac), according to JLL Malaysia's 2Q2026 report.

business.title: Oil Prices Jump Again as U.S.-Iran Talks Appear Deadlocked
business.description: Investors parsed reports that President Trump told advisers that he was not satisfied with Iran’s latest proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

business.title: New Chip Can Protect Wireless Biomedical Devices From Quantum Attacks

business.title: Serstech secures 10 MSEK loan from Almi
business.description: Serstech has entered into a loan agreement with Almi for 10 MSEK. The proceeds will be used to build inventory of components and finished products to support anticipated demand. The loan carries an interest rate of 8.12% and matures on 31 December 2026.

science.description: Scientists have discovered the remains of a 450-million-year-old jellyfish. The discovery is rare as soft-bodied animals are not easily preserved as fossils.

ai.title: CIA has your fingerprints if you used ChatGPT palm reader, meme after viral moment reflects real anxiety
ai.description: Did you ask ChatGPT to do your palm or face analysis? If you have, the internet says the CIA now has access to your biometric details like fingerprints and face scans. It is an outlandish claim, of course. But it reflects a real anxiety about privacy and AI tools like ChatGPT.

ai.title: The silent shift: How AI could redraw India’s employment map

ai.title: ideaForge inks pact with Japan's DMP to develop next-gen AI-powered drones
ai.description: ideaForges vision is to build next-generation intelligent unmanned aircraft systems by seamlessly integrating advanced design, engineering, manufacturing capabilities, and AI technologies, said Ankit Mehta, CEO and Co-founder, ideaForge Technology Ltd. Through this collaboration, ideaForge would be able to leverage DMPs strong presence and understanding of the Japanese market to accelerate the companys global expansion initiatives and will provide smarter, more efficient, and sustainable autonomous drone systems that deliver real-time decision-making, extended endurance, and unmatched reliability in the most demanding environments, he added.
